LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF REGINALD HINDLEY'S MOTION FOR ORDER VACATING AND SETTING ASIDE DISMISSAL AND RELATED ORDERS. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE CASE WAS RESOLVED AFTER PLAINTIFF'S CONVERSATION WITH MR. EMILSON. THE DISMISSAL IS VACATED AS IS THE ORDER GRANTING THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. COURT DENIED DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO RE-SET NEW HEARING ON SLAPP MOTION; DEFENDANT TO RE-FILE, IF SO CHOOSES. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Vacating And Setting Aside Dismissal And Related Orders
Off Calendar Oct-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF REGINALD HINDLEY'S Motion For Order Vacating And Setting Aside Dismissal And Related Orders.
APPEARANCES: Kayla M. Grant, Esq. 309-5248 specially appearing for Reginald Hindley, 707/575-3700 pltf & moving party; Fredrik Emilson, Esq. of Cornerstone Law Group 357-2099 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE CASE WAS RESOLVED AFTER PLAINTIFF'S CONVERSATION WITH MR. EMILSON. THE DISMISSAL IS VACATED AS IS THE ORDER GRANTING THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. Court denied defendant's request to re-set new hearing on SLAPP motion; defendant to re-file, if so chooses. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
